hypothesis: For Task 6 hidden size 64 lanes cached single-token attention is much smaller than the tied-vocab output head in MACs and bytes, so attention should become a bounded next kernel only after Python calibration recovers while full TinyStories output projection remains the first streaming pressure.

tooling_constraint.python_failure: bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
tooling_constraint.artifact_generation_method: manual JSON scorecard from checked-in model configs and explicit arithmetic, written with sed w because apply_patch and Python are unavailable

assumptions.dsp_lane_cycle_model: ceil MACs divided by DSP lanes, excluding softmax non-MAC latency and control overhead
assumptions.attention_mode: single-token causal decode with a cached K/V window
assumptions.softmax_model: score scratch counted as int16 logits only; exact exp-divide approximation cost is not included

decision.recommendation: Do not start a large synthesis run. Once Python works, create a deterministic cached-attention calibration artifact for v1k, v4k, and full-shape windows, then cut a one-layer v4k attention RTL stub only if qkv, score, softmax-value, output projection, and residual errors stay inside explicit thresholds.
decision.first_attention_cutout_boundary: single-layer hidden64 cached causal attention with window64, int8 qkv and output projections, int16 score accumulation, streamed per-head softmax-value accumulation, output projection, and post-attention residual int8 boundary; no vocab logits and no board programming
decision.why_not_defer_for_resources: At v4k, attention is about 9.4 percent of the output-head 4-lane cycle count and about 10.0 percent of rowwise int8 top-k stream bytes. At full TinyStories, all-layer cached attention is about 12.2 percent of output-head cycles, so output projection remains the first scaling pressure.
decision.why_not_promote_to_rtl_now: The softmax approximation and multi-sample quantization behavior need Python model-trace validation, and this invocation cannot run Python.
